svg.text: Sort Word Syllables — every word gets its whole‑word profile (CVC, CVCV, …), needed before any segmental sort
STAGE 1 — confirm the three basics for every word: word‑initial C/V · word‑final C/V · syllable count
Read this list aloud. Click on any that are NOT consonant initial.
asked when the list being checked is a
syllable‑count list (instead of a direct move)
any word that does NOT belong
the word MOVES to its correct group
OK: These are all consonant initial
then ask again, from the top
All the syllable groups are checked! You can
now sort the words by syllable profile.
STAGE 2 — sort whole words into profiles, one class at a time (C2V = C‑initial · 2 syllables · V‑final)
…belongs in which group? (by Whole Citation Word Syllable Profile)
Set a C2V profile by hand
⚠ a linguist’s call — work with your language team.
must be C‑initial, V‑final, 2 syllable(s) — type C and V (e.g. CVCV)
beside it: “Already in play here — don’t re‑enter these:”
This word doesn’t belong in this C2V profile at all…
each choice re‑files the word into that class,
Read this list aloud. Click on any that are NOT CVCV.
any word that is NOT CVCV
it leaves CVCV — back onto the pile
(no printed question — are these two profiles really one?)
We are joining these profiles; which is correct?
the words of the profile NOT chosen re‑annotate
to the chosen one — which needs re‑verifying
All ‘C2V’ groups in the ‘lc’ check are verified and distinct!
Moving on to the next profile!
next profile class — again from the top
every word now has a profile — go sort segments (column 2)
sort words by one sound at a time — example: Sort Vowels (Nouns · profile CVC · check V1 ‘First Vowel’)
rows = profiles · columns = checks · ✓ done · ▣ where you are now
Some of these words have no syllable profile yet, so they won’t be sorted here either.
dɔ̀k (#C=C C#=C syls=1) → CVC · … (each affected word is listed)
Trust machine analysis; maybe correct later
Sort syllable profiles manually; come back here later
continue with only the words that already have profiles
one button per existing group; 🔃 = change its example word (right‑click backs up)
This word has First Vowel / Other V1
one button: “This word has First Vowel” for the very first word; “Other V1” after
this word’s syllable profile is cleared —
checked once, never shown as a group
cleared words return to syllable sorting (column 1)
Read this list aloud. Click on any with a different First Vowel sound.
if the group being read is NA:
“Read this list aloud, and click on any
OK: “These all DO NOT have First Vowel”
NA holds the skipped words; it is checked
once, but never shown as a group
any word with a different sound
that word leaves ‘a’ — back onto the
OK: These all have the same First Vowel (V1)
(no printed question — do these two have the same V1 sound?)
“Now we’re going to move group ‘1’ into ‘a’,
the merged ‘a’ will be re‑read (verify, above)
file the sound groups under letters (column 3)
All ‘CVC’ groups in the ‘V1’ check are verified and distinct!
Moving on to the next check! / Moving on to the next profile!
(the next check / profile is selected automatically)
again, with the next check / profile
nothing left → back to the task window
file the verified sound groups under letters; name the letters; every word is respelled
groups already spelled with an existing letter
are filed under it (when there’s no conflict)
the item is a whole sound group:
one button per letter: the big letter (or ‘?’ if unnamed) + one member’s example
the group is filed under letter ‘a’
if ‘a’ already holds a group from the same check,
the two are un‑distinguished and must be
conflicting pair → compared again, from the top of column 2
a NEW unnamed letter is made
Read this list aloud. Note where each was sorted (V1, V2).
Click on any with a different Vowel sound in that place.
one row per member sound group
any group with a different sound
the pile of groups to file
OK: These all should use the same letter (currently ‘a’)
(no printed question — one letter, or two?)
the two letters merge into one — the
merged letter must be re‑read (✓ removed)
What letter(s) will you use for this Vowel group?
palette: a e i o u ɛ ɔ ə ɨ ʉ æ ã ɛ̃ ɔ̃ …
Don’t use Other Groups: a e i o u
feedback: “Give a name for this group!” · “Sorry, there is already a group
with that label; see comparison below.”
OK: Add the letter ‘ɛ’ to my alphabet for this sound
(the OK text re‑reads whatever you type)
What other Vowel do you want to work with?
its example appears beside yours, with
the two groups trade letters — the traded one then needs its own name
Go back and join with one of ← these groups
this letter can be compared again
every word’s written form is rewritten with its new letters
if a name is still contested:
“Some groups can’t take their glyph name yet — another group in the
same slice is still using that name. We’ll try again later:”
